title: identity — HTTP routes
description: This module publishes NO HTTP routes. Every authentication endpoint (login, refresh, logout, password reset, email verification, MFA challenge, cross-app SSO grant) lives in the `identity/auth` module which composes over this substrate. See design.md §7 for the full HTTP contract.
rationale: The `identity` module is the SECURITY SUBSTRATE. Exposing HTTP routes on it would blur the boundary between the credential-storage layer and the authentication protocol.

Every operation on an Identity row transits through auth-service actions: LoginAction / ChallengeMfaAction / RequestPasswordResetAction / CompletePasswordResetAction / VerifyEmailAction / ResendEmailVerificationAction / RegisterAction. Those actions live in `identity/auth`.

Platform-admin CRUD on the Identity row (list / describe / unlock / force-reset / erase) is exposed through Artisan commands (see commands.json). The platform-admin HTTP surface — `/api/v1/platform/identities/*` — also lives in `identity/auth` where the routing + guard wiring belongs.
